title: Sickingia salvadorensis
taxonomy: Sickingia salvadorensis is a species of flowering plant in the family Podostemaceae. It is native to the Americas. The plant's classification is as follows: kingdom Plantae, clade Angiosperms, clade Eudicots, clade Asterids, and order Malpighiales. Within the family Podostemaceae, Sickingia salvadorensis is part of the tribe Podostemateae. The plant's specific classification is Sickingia salvadorensis, a monotypic genus within the family.

Additionally, Sickingia salvadorensis may possess neuroprotective properties, potentially helping to prevent or manage neurodegenerative diseases such as Alzheimer's and Parkinson's. Further research is needed to fully understand the health benefits of Sickingia salvadorensis.
